Football.title: Roy Keane rants why he WOULDN’T appoint Michael Carrick as permanent Man Utd boss – and names two top picks
Football.content:
ROY KEANE launched a stinging rant to explain why he would NOT make Michael Carrick the permanent Manchester United manager.

And then he revealed who his two top picks for the Old Trafford hotseat are.





Carrick took over on a short-term deal to the end of the season when Ruben Amorim was sacked in January.

He has overseen seven wins, one draw and one defeat in his nine matches.

The ex-Boro boss maintained his 100 per cent winning record at home with Sunday’s 3-1 win over Aston Villa to take another step closer to Champions League qualification.

After the game on Sky Sports, Keane was asked if he would give Carrick the gig beyond the summer given his impressive spell.

But he was emphatic in his response.

The no-nonsense Irishman simply replied: “No.”

Asked to elaborate, Keane continued: “I think there’s better options out there.

“The games where he’s come in, in terms of winning football matches, he’s done a very good job.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

“But there’s been no jeopardy in those games, he’s had free weeks to prepare for the matches, there’s been good timing for him to go in there, and he’s done a very good job. I take my hat off to him.

“If he does get the job in the summer, you say, ‘Good luck to you,’ but I think there are far better options out there for the next manager of Manchester United.

“If Carrick had been mentioned three months ago to be the next United manager, you’d have been locked up. We would have said he wasn’t even the reckoning.

“He’s done a very good job but I think there’s better options out there, simple as that.

“United will have to interview people and see what’s out there.”



Keane went on to explain that there is a big difference between an inteirm or temporary manager and getting the job full-time, where the gaffer is involved in decisions over contracts, medical department and transfers.

He then explained who and what he is looking for when United do get around to appointing their new boss.

Keane added: “I think to manage Manchester United, you need someone with a lot more experience in terms of winning trophies, competing in Europe, and he hasn’t got that. It might happen in a few years, and he might be in the mix then.

“I remember this is the same group of players that finished 15th. So everyone else can get giddy about United being third in the league. I don’t. I’m not one of them.

“If people honestly think Carrick’s the best option… there’s loads of options out there.



“You go and get the best. Carrick’s not the best option for Man United.”

Pressed for names, Keane admitted Thomas Tuchel would be under consideration but his new England contract until 2028 rules him out.

Instead, he identified Diego Simeone and Luis Enrique, at Atletico Madrid and PSG respectively.

Man Utd player ratings vs Aston Villa BRUNO FERNANDES and Casemiro boosted Manchester United’s Champions League dreams with a 3-1 win over top-five rivals Aston Villa. Here’s how SunSport’s Katherine Walsh rated the United players from Old Trafford… Senne Lammens: Lammens was left vulnerable by his defense and failed to produce the kind of performance that would catch the eye of the watching Peter Schmeichel. 6 Diogo Dalot: Dalot combined promising energy with a glaring lack of composure, most notably when he inexplicably skied a point-blank sitter before the break. 6 Harry Maguire: Maguire was a dominant physical presence who effectively nullified Ollie Watkins, even while remaining frustratingly prone to his trademark shakiness. 7 Leny Yoro: Yoro staged a heroic redemption from his previous “disaster-class” with a sensational, last-ditch slide tackle to deny a certain goal. 7 Luke Shaw: Despite being weak for the cross, Shaw likely secured his place in Thomas Tuchel’s World Cup plans with several vital, game-saving blocks. 7 Casemiro: The Brazilian legend turned his farewell parade into a masterclass, proving his irreplaceable intelligence as fans pleaded for “one more year.” 8 Kobbie Mainoo: Mainoo snapped out of his recent slump with a display of pure composure, highlighted by a massive, goal-saving challenge inside the box. 7 Amad: The diminutive winger rose highest for a spectacular header that forced a world-class save, though his impact was marred by sloppy distribution. 7 Bruno Fernandes: Fernandes cemented his status as the league’s best No. 10 by shattering David Beckham’s club record with a pair of sublime assists. 9 Matheus Cunha: Cunha’s feisty spirit and clinical finish allowed him to “stick the knife in” Unai Emery with a provocative surfing celebration. 8 Bryan Mbeumo: Mbeumo’s form continues its worrying downward spiral, as he struggled to produce anything memorable beyond winning a single corner. 6 For a deeper dive into each player and SunSport’s take on the subs, check out the full player ratings.

Keane reasoned: “It’s like when you’re looking for a midfielder as a club. You go and try and get the best midfielder. You don’t get one who’s maybe come on loan for five, six games.

“If they go out and try and suss these managers out and they all come back and they want 25, 30 million, here you go, I get it.



“But the responsibility of the board surely is to go, let’s go and get the best that’s available to Manchester United. That’s it.

“If Carrick gets the job in the summer, what’s his remit? I’m guessing to win the league title in the next year or two. I’m pretty sure it would be.

“Are we all going to sit here and go, ‘Yeah, we think he’s capable of doing it’? I don’t think he is. That experience must come into it.

“If Carrick’s the manager in the summer and he’s coming back and Casemiro’s gone and he’s making decisions on Harry Maguire, it’s a different vibe at the club then.

“They won’t be seeing him as this nice guy. He’ll have to make tough decisions. That’s where the challenge is coming from.



“But if he does all that and he produces and he starts winning trophies the next year or two, I’ll tell you what, I didn’t see that coming.

“Carlo Ancelotti for example. If Ancelotti walks in the summer and he’s chatting to the players, I’m sure they’re going, ‘This is a guy who’s won loads of stuff.’

“I played under Brian Clough and Alex Ferguson. When they’re coming to your dressing room, ‘Oh yeah, I like this, now you’re talking, we’re going places with this guy.’

“Has Carrick got that huge respect amongst the players? Maybe they have because he’s a nice guy and all this. But I’m on about bringing a CV with you.

“I’m not saying Carrick and his staff aren’t qualified to do sessions. Of course they are. He has brought experienced coaches with him. I get that.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

“He’s proven he can win a few football matches. Let’s see if he can win some titles.

“But if you ask me, do I think United will get back to winning league titles the next year or two with Carrick and his staff? The answer is no. But I’d love to be proven wrong.”

Football.title: ‘Someone please help me’ – Former Liverpool and England star left stunned by what Arne Slot did versus Tottenham
Football.content:
FORMER Liverpool star Jermaine Pennant was left begging for help as he tried to understand a hat-trick of tactical decisions made by Arne Slot.

Liverpool were held in a frustrating 1-1 draw against relegation-battlers Tottenham at Anfield.





Manager Slot named a rotated XI without Mohamed Salah in it, and played Rio Ngumoha from the start for his full Premier League debut.

Dominik Szoboszlai opened the scoring with a trademark free-kick, though Jamie Carragher berated Guglielmo Vicario on Sky Sports co-comms for his poor attempt at a save.

But Spurs eventually hit a late equaliser through former Everton man Richarlison to take a deserved point back to London.

With Aston Villa and Chelsea both losing this weekend, the Reds actually gained ground on the top four Champions League spots, although it was still very much two points lost rather than a point gained.



Ex-Premier League and England Under-21 star Pennant echoed that sentiment with a scorching statement on social media.

The 43-year-old was desperate to try and understand some of Slot’s decisions and took to X, formerly known as Twitter, to vent his frustrations.

He said: “Someone please help me, help me understand.

“Help me understand why 1 ) [Hugo] Ekitike doesn’t start, 2 [he] takes Rio off leave, [Cody] Gakpo on, and 3) [He] takes [Jeremie] Frimpong off and then leaves Szoboszlai at RB…”



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Slot had brought Hugo Ekitike on for Ngumoha in the 65th minute in a triple sub that also saw Florian Wirtz subbed off for Curtis Jones and Jeremie Frimpong taken off for Salah.

Trey Nyoni, 18, made only his fourth appearance of the season off the bench in place of Cody Gakpo, only for Spurs to score six minutes later.

Federico Chiesa came on for Alexis Mac Allister in injury time, but it was too little too late.

Pennant issued a response to one fan who tried to answer his query by saying Mac Allister was the correct sub earlier due to how easily the Argentine was being run past.

He said: “But if we [can see it], and I’m guessing most of the fans would agree, how can the man in charge not see this.



“There has to be s*** going on behind the scenes that we have no clue about, cos this is blasphemy.”

Pennant played 81 times for Liverpool in his career, including 55 Prem games in which he scored three goals.

Throughout his career, the winger also played for the likes of Arsenal, Birmingham and Stoke.

Pennant never played senior football for the Three Lions, and holds the bizarre record of being one of only two Englishmen to appear in the Champions League final and not for the national team, the other being Jamie Gittens.

Football.title: Dominik Szoboszlai fumes Liverpool need to ‘wake up’ or risk ending up in Conference League as champions booed off
Football.content:
DOMINIK SZOBOSZLAI admitted Liverpool are more likely to end up in the Conference League than the Champions League right now.

The Kop certainly thinks so, booing off the fallen champs for failing to beat the worst Tottenham team in decades.





Arne Slot’s under-achievers are back at Anfield on Wednesday, when they must beat Galatasaray to stay in the Champions League.

Liverpool are fifth in the Premier League – which should be good enough for Champions League qualification.

But goal-scorer Szoboszlai warned that if they didn’t buck up their ideas they would find themselves in Europe’s least important competition instead – and be grateful not to have missed out altogether.

He said: “We have to wake up because if we carry on like this, next season we should be happy if we play in the Conference League.



“I don’t know why this is happening, I honestly don’t know. If I knew the answer I would tell the guys.”

Szoboszlai, who saw his free-kick opener cancelled out by Richarlison in stoppage time, did not blame the fans for booing.

But he insisted the players still deserved support because of last season’s heroics.

He said: “I didn’t hear the boos, but we can understand them – we are not performing how we should.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

“But they should stand behind us because last season everybody was happy. So support us now when we are in a difficult time.”

Slot described the fan frustration as “completely normal”.

He said: “I’m 100% sure that if they show up on Wednesday that they will be supportive again from the start.

“But if the team doesn’t perform to the standards that they are used to it is completely normal that they are frustrated.”

Many Liverpool fans want Slot sacked despite last season’s success but former Kop star Jamie Carragher blamed the new signings instead.



He said: “Could a new manager completely revitalise these type of players and bring that energy and sort of intensity back into this team? I’m not sure.

“I think a lot of it goes back to what happened in the summer, the profile of the players that came in.

“There’s just too many players who just want to play when the ball’s at their feet. They don’t want to dig in and make it difficult.

“That’s why it’s so easy to play against this Liverpool team.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Galatasaray – 1-0 winners in the last-16 first leg, warmed up with a 3-0 win at home to Istanbul Basaksehir in the Turkish Süper Lig.



Star striker Victor Osimhen was on target – and worryingly for Liverpool has has now either scored or assisted in his last nine domestic games.

Football.title: How Arsenal starlet Max Dowman became Prem’s youngest scorer & problem he must overcome to play for England in World Cup
Football.content:
AFTER calmly rolling the ball into the back of the net to become the youngest ever scorer in the Premier League, Arsenal’s Max Dowman jogged down the touchline as if it were a walk in the park.

His manager, on the other hand, leaped for joy and the 16-year-old midfielder’s teammates chased him to the corner flag and buried him with their fevered celebrations on Saturday.





Max is used to breaking records.

Last year, the schoolboy, who is taking his GCSEs in summer, became the youngest player to appear in the Champions League and the youngest to score for England’s Under-19s.

The way things are going, he could also be the youngest player to be capped for the Three Lions.

Aged 16 years and 73 days when he scored on the weekend against Everton, Max is so young that he was not even allowed to get changed with the senior squad due to safeguarding protocols.

But he has remained remarkably unflustered during his meteoric rise.

Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta said: “A tiny smile, that’s what you get with him. For him, everything is natural, for him, everything is OK.

“It’s the way he plays and that’s the secret, that he doesn’t make a big fuss of it.”

Everyone in the beautiful game realises Max is something special.



Former England captain John Terry said: “I watched him play against Chelsea about a year ago and I’ve not seen anyone glide past people like this man does other than Messi.

“That’s a big, big comparison, but this man is a proper talent and will play a big part in Arsenal’s future, and England’s.”

It is not uncommon for a fresh talent to be described as the new Lionel Messi, but this time the comparison to Argentina’s superstar forward is not misplaced.

Before Max came on as a 74th- minute substitute at the Emirates Stadium, the game looked to be heading towards a drab 0-0 draw, which would have dented the Gunners’ hopes of winning the league. The teen changed all that.

Max had set up Arsenal’s opener with a delightful cross.



Then, in the 97th minute, after taking the ball from well inside his own half and skipping past two opponents, he scored the history-making clincher — a contender for Goal Of The Season.

It broke the 20-year record held by James Vaughan, who scored for Everton against Crystal Palace aged 16 years and 270 days in April 2005.

Last night, on social media, fans were already calling for England manager Thomas Tuchel to include Max in his squad for this summer’s World Cup.



If Max does make his international debut this year, he will break the record held by former Arsenal and England wonder kid Theo Walcott, 37, who was capped at the tender age of 17 years and 75 days in 2006.

Theo, who knows better than most about what Max is experiencing, commented: “There was a lot of weight. I went to the World Cup without even playing in the league, that brings a different type of pressure. He’s going to face pressure every day now.

“Arsenal look after him. But you’ve got a sense he’s got a good head on his shoulders. I think the world is at his feet.”

It is in part thanks to his grounded family that Max is so level-headed.

His dad Rob, who founded his own insurance company, and mum Caroline have kept him out of the limelight.



Max, who lives with his parents in Essex, has not given any interviews or appeared on any red carpets.

This month, though, he did star in an advert for Adidas Predator football boots with former Arsenal legend Ian Wright playing his teacher.

That would have brought in some welcome cash.

Before he turned 16, Max was only on £350 a week at the North London club and can not sign a professional contract until he is 17.

He attends school when possible, trains with Arsenal’s first team during the day and has tutoring in the evening.



His parents are keen for him to keep up with his studies and get good grades in his GCSEs.

Nick Hutt, who is the chairman of Billericay Town Colts, where Max first laced up his boots, said: “Max has always been kind of old for his age. Very mature, laid-back and he worked incredibly hard at his football. They’re a very grounded family. His mum, Caroline, and Rob, they keep Max grounded.

“They’re very humble, nice people, very kind of community-focused. So I’m sure they’ve played an important role in getting him ready for where he is now.”



Rob was part of a consortium that helped rescue Billericay Town, who are in the seventh tier of English football, from a financial crisis in 2019.

Max’s dad used to play for the team, as did his grandfather.

Nick said: “Max’s mum Caroline would help out at the club and would work in the club shop.

“His older siblings Ethan and Imogen worked behind the bar when they were students, too, so there’s quite a long-standing connection with the family.”

Rob was coaching one of the many youth teams when he brought his then five-year-old son along for a Saturday morning kickabout.



Former Billericay Town youth team coach Ross Poyton saw how talented Max was, even at that young age.

He said: “The ball came into this little, blond, left-footed boy, who popped it round one lad’s legs and ran round the other side. It was a move that a player five years older would make. Then, just to show it wasn’t a fluke, he did it again, and it was like ‘Wow’.

“I asked someone else what the boy’s name was and then they told me he was actually a year younger than all the other boys he was playing.”

Max joined Arsenal’s youth academy soon after and has continued to play with kids much older than him.

Aged 13, he was the youngest player to turn out for Arsenal’s Under-18s and, a year later, he was the youngest to debut for their Under-21s side.



He had a mentor in Arsenal’s youth set-up, who understood what it is like to be the next big thing.

Jack Wilshere was the academy coach who brought Max on in an Under-18s game when he was 13.

The ex-Arsenal midfielder said: “He came on and the first thing he did, he ran around three players, squared it and we scored.”

Jack, who was compared to Paul Gascoigne when he earned his first England cap aged 18 and 222 days in 2010, believes Max is even more talented than he was.



He added: “People ask, ‘Is he better than you?’. He is better than me. But what I see in similarities is his love of the game.

“I worked with some kids who wouldn’t even watch games, but he loves it.”

All the top clubs would love to sign Max when he turns 17 on New Year’s Eve.

But the family are all Arsenal fans and he has committed his future to the North London club.

Rob and older brother Ethan, who is registered as a soccer agent, will surely be able to strike a blockbuster deal.



Even then, Max is unlikely to be on the front of glossy magazines.

The desire to wrap Max up in cotton wool is understandable.

Football is littered with tales of players going off the rails, from George Best to Gascoigne.

Spanish superstar Lamine Yamal, who made his debut for Barcelona aged 15 years and 285 days in 2023, landed in hot water last year for his partying.

There was outrage when it was reported that he had hired people with dwarfism to perform at his 18th birthday bash.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

Wilshere, whose own career was cut short by injuries, said: “The most important thing with Max is he’s a top kid.

“He’s got a good family. He’s got a mum who is amazing and a dad who would do anything for him and wants the best for his son.”

Football.title: Mikel Arteta turned to a schoolboy in Arsenal’s hour of need – but Max Dowman brilliance is no shock to his u12s coach
Football.content:
IT was a gut feeling that led Mikel Arteta to turn to Max Dowman and his “super strength” in Arsenal’s hour of need.

But the way the 16-year-old lit up the Emirates on Saturday night means Arteta will surely soon be picking Dowman because of what his head tells him.





There were so many remarkable elements to Dowman’s goal against Everton that secured a 2-0 victory and helped the Gunners go nine points clear at the top of the Premier League.

Chief among them is the fact the forward became the youngest goalscorer in Prem history at the age of just 16 years and 73 days, beating the record set by the Toffees’ James Vaughan in April 2005 (16 years and 270 days).

Then there was the manner of the goal.

Deep into injury time, whizkid Dowman picked up the ball just outside his own penalty area, skipped past two Everton players and ran the length of the pitch to score into an empty net.



It was the moment the teenager announced himself to the world with the type of goal those in the club’s Hale End academy had seen him score before.

Temisan Williams, who coached Dowman when he was with Arsenal’s Under-12s, is one of them and believes this is just the start.

Speaking exclusively to SunSport, Williams said: “Carrying the ball has always been Max’s super strength from his academy days.

“He has always been able to carry the ball forward 40-50 yards.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

“And to see him doing it on the biggest stage is the reason why it’s so important to help young players to keep excelling at what they are best at.

“Max has the capabilities, as we have seen, to go to the highest level.”

The Full Arsenal on YouTube SunSport have launched a dedicated Arsenal channel on YouTube! The Full Arsenal Podcast is your home for everything about the Gunners. From match reactions and tactical breakdowns to transfer talk, match previews, tactical analysis and honest fan debate… The Full Arsenal covers it all. Subscribe to The Full Arsenal here

Dowman’s age and the nature of his goal were striking — but so was the fact Arteta threw him on.

Few would label Arsenal’s boss a gambler, yet with the match at 0-0 and the title race at a defining moment, he turned to a schoolboy.

The safe option would have been to bring on Gabriel Jesus, who is the only player in the Gunners squad to have won the Prem, helping Manchester City to lift four during his time at the Etihad.



But Arteta plumped for Dowman with his side desperately needing a spark. It was an inspired call.

Despite coming on with only 16 minutes to go, the winger finished the game tied first among Arsenal players for chances created from open play, dribbles completed and shots on target.

Arteta pleaded for calm when asked if Dowman would now be starting matches.

However, Saturday underlined how he has a role to play as the season enters crunch time. Arsenal host Bayer Leverkusen in the second leg of their Champions League last-16 tie on Tuesday.

It is 1-1 after last week’s match in Germany and, if the Gunners need a goal, Arteta should now have no fear about turning to Dowman.

Even against City in Sunday’s Carabao Cup final at Wembley, unleashing Dowman does not feel like a wild call.

The talent is clearly there and he even supplied the cross that eventually led to Viktor Gyokeres opening the scoring against Everton.

Sweden forward Gyokeres, 27, said: “When you see him play, you don’t think that he’s 16.

“You don’t think about it when he has the ball with the composure and the confidence he plays with.

“It’s incredible. He makes a lot of right decisions when he comes on the pitch.”



Things have always come quickly for Dowman. At the age of four he was scouted by John Knight, who had him drafted into the Gunners’ pre-academy sessions.

The Dowman family have always been grateful to Knight and he was there when their lad signed his pre-contract agreement in January.

That deal was done ahead of Dowman penning professional terms when he turns 17 in December and you wonder how much more he could achieve before then.



Saturday was another record to add to his countless others, such as being the youngest-ever player to appear in the Champions League.

He is now also the youngest Gunner to find the net, beating Cesc Fabregas to that honour by 139 days.

One of the challenges for Arteta will be managing the hype around Dowman, although he himself has likened the kid to Lionel Messi.

Last week a mural of Dowman appeared outside the Emirates alongside one of legendary striker Dennis Bergkamp.

And he has also starred in an advert for adidas with another club icon, Ian Wright.



Arteta believes the network around Dowman will help.

He has a supportive family and big-name stars like Bukayo Saka, Declan Rice and Martin Odegaard are mentoring him, too.

Arteta added: “His family and all the people at Hale End have raised him in such an incredible way.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“One thing is to raise a talent, another is to raise a person with such a personality and charisma to play a game with that stature in the manner that he’s done it.

“For many years, we will remember we were at the Emirates that night when that 16-year-old scored in such an important game when we were trying to win the title.”

Football.title: ‘I did not say that’ – Igor Tudor snaps at reporter in extraordinary interview before asking ‘are we finished’
Football.content:
IGOR TUDOR cut his post-match interview with Sky Sports short after getting involved in a tense stand-off following Tottenham’s stalemate at Liverpool.

Spurs have found themselves in serious danger of suffering a humiliating relegation from the Premier League.





But Tottenham gave themselves fresh hope of avoiding the drop thanks to Richarlison grabbing a last-gasp equaliser in the 1-1 draw at Anfield.

It allowed Tudor, 47, to end a run of four straight defeats since he was appointed as Thomas Frank’s successor until the end of the season.

But the Croat sensationally snapped at Sky Sports reporter Patrick Davison when he was asked whether the point would give Spurs fans hope of staying in the Premier League.

Tudor, who was blasted for his treatment of keeper Antonin Kinsky during the drubbing at Atletico Madrid earlier this week, said: “It’s enough with this. I always tell you what I think.”



Davison then asked: “But do you not think sometimes that these questions need to be asked and need to be answered? You’ve got a lot of Spurs fans all around the world.”

In double quick time, Tudor fired back with: “What questions need to be answered?”

And Davison clarified: “Well, about the future of the club. About who is going to be the manager. Is the team going to stay in the league?”

Tudor jumped in, stating: “That’s not a question for me. I’m the coach. You need to ask me about players, how we play.”



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

Davison then injected, commenting: “I have.”

But Tudor hit back, adding: “And I’ve answered you, so it’s a question that doesn’t have sense.



“When you ask the coach about their position, we don’t have any sense of this.

“All the coaches will say the same things. But you always ask this so the coach needs to answer something because he’s obliged to come to this press conference.

“Otherwise he would like to stay at home. But you insist almost always with the same questions.”

Davison, trying to move the interview along, claimed: “But the point being you can provide the hope, right. You said in an interview on Friday.”

The under-fire Tottenham chief insisted: “No, I did not say that.”



But Davison continued: “You said people need hope of a new manager sometimes.

“And my point is, have you shown today with the way Tottenham have played that you can provide hope?”

Tudor doubled down, saying: “What kind of question is this? What do I need to tell you now?”

Davison answered: “You can say whatever you like.”

A disgruntled Tudor commented: “I said this. What will I tell my players? Of course. This is the answer you want to hear.”



And Davison repeated: “You can say whatever you like.”

Tudor then briefly looked away from the camera before asking: “Have we finished or not?”

Davison conceded: “We’re finished.”

Tudor was then seen storming off before Sky Sports’ coverage panned to presenter Kelly Cates, who was sitting alongside pundits Roy Keane, Ashley Young and Jamie Redknapp.

They agreed that the bizarre interaction could have been the result of a communication breakdown.



The interview divided opinion on social media, with some blasting Tudor and others suggesting that Davison was wrong to continue questioning the ex-Juventus boss over his future.

One wrote: “He managed a draw in one match and has the ego of a world-class coach.”

But a viewer sitting on the other side of the fence claimed: “That interview was disgraceful. Sky should be ashamed.”

Another chimed in: “A ridiculous set of questions. Should have just walked away rather than give him the dignity of a response.”

A third added: “Completely right of Tudor to call out the pointlessness of that line of questioning.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

“What is the interviewer expecting of him? It’s purely to try and rile him and get a viral clip out of it, he’s done that but most people are instead siding with Tudor over it.”

A fourth commented: “Igor Tudor does not hold back. That post-match energy is peak spicy. Love how he keeps it real with the media, straight to the point!”

Football.title: Baran Vardar dead at 20: Turkish starlet passes away after long battle with cancer as club say ‘we’ll never forget you’
Football.content:
TRAGEDY Baran Vardar dead at 20: Turkish starlet passes away after long battle with cancer as club say ‘we’ll never forget you’

TURKISH side Istanbulspor are mourning following the death of Baran Vardar at the age of 20.

The highly-rated centre-back passed away following a lengthy battle with cancer.





Vardar was forced to put his hopes of remaining in the first-team picture on hold when he had to undergo treatment.

And Istanbulspor, who compete in Turkey’s second tier, confirmed that the youngster had died.

A club statement, posted on X, read: “We are in great pain and deep sorrow.

“We are experiencing profound grief over the loss of our footballer, our dear Baran Alp Vardar, who has been undergoing cancer treatment for some time.

“Baran Alp, with his character, loyalty to his team-mates and cheerful smile, will always be remembered.

“He will forever live in the hearts of our community. His departure from us at such a young age has deeply wounded us all.

“We pray to Allah for mercy upon our late footballer Baran Alp Vardar, and offer patience and condolences to his precious family first and foremost, as well as to his loved ones, team-mates and our community.

“May your resting place be paradise, Baran Alp… We will never forget you.



“The funeral of our dear Baran Alp will take place after the funeral prayer to be performed at Beylikduzu Mevlana Mosque following the noon prayer on Sunday.”

Vardar made three appearances for Istanbulspor when they were in the Super Lig during the 2023-24 season.

He came off the bench in defeats to Alanyaspor and Sivasspor before being rewarded with his first start against Fenerbahce in May 2024.

But Vardar did not make another senior outing for his boyhood club as his career was put on hold.

Istanbulspor are currently 14th in the 20-team Trendyol 1. Lig, having won nine of their 31 games.

Football.title: Chelsea icon Thiago Silva flies back to Brazil and shares heartbreaking message after tragic death of his mum
Football.content:
CHELSEA legend Thiago Silva flew back to Brazil following the heartbreaking death of his mother.

Silva, 41, took to social media on Sunday to reveal the sad news.





The Brazilian defender posted a series of photos including selfies with his mum Angela.

Other pictures appeared to be of her friends and family mourning her tragic passing at the funeral in his homeland.

Silva paid an emotional tribute to his doting mother with a heartwrenching message.

He wrote: “Rest in peace, my mother!



“I will miss you so much.

“Watch over us from above.

“I will love you forever my crown.”

Various team-mates from throughout his career replied with messages of support, including Willian, Felipe Melo, Marco Verratti and Chelsea captain Reece James.



The Stamford Bridge club also took to X with a classy post.

The club typed: “Everyone at Chelsea FC is deeply saddened to learn of the passing of Angela Maria da Silva, beloved mother of Thiago Silva.

“Our thoughts are with Thiago, and all of Angela’s family and friends at this difficult time.”

Silva spent four years at Chelsea after shining for AC Milan and PSG.

He was part of Thomas Tuchel’s team that won the Champions League in 2021.



The 113-cap Brazil icon left in 2024 for Fluminense but is now back in Europe after joining Porto in December, making his debut the following month.

His appearance, starting in a 1-0 cup victory over rivals Benfica, saw him overtake Pepe as Porto’s oldest player.

Football.title: Ashley Cole lands first job in management with shock switch to second-tier club
Football.content:
Ashley Cole lands first job in management with shock switch to second-tier club

Ashley Cole has been learning his trade with the likes of Derby County, Everton, Chelsea, Birmingham City and various England teams, and now he's become a head coach

View Image Premier League icon Ashley Cole has taken a job in Italy (Image: CESENA FC)

Ashley Cole has been appointed the new manager of Italian side Cesena. Cole, 45, joins the Serie B club on a deal until the end of this season.

‌



The move marks Cole's first head coach position, having learned his trade at various clubs and with the FA. The 107-time England international worked with Frank Lampard at Chelsea and Everton, and alongside Wayne Rooney at Birmingham City.

‌



Cole also assisted Lee Carsley during his time as England's interim manager. In September 2024, the ex-Arsenal and Chelsea left-back was appointed a full-time national coach by the FA.

‌



Now Cole has signed a short-term contract with Cesena, who sit eighth in the Italian second tier, occupying the league's final play-off spot. The club sacked Michele Mignani on Saturday announced Cole's appointment on Sunday.

FOLLOW OUR CHELSEA FB PAGE! Latest Blues news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Liam Rosenior to hold urgent PGMOL talks after Chelsea referee incident in Newcastle loss READ MORE :

A statement on Cesena's website read: "The entire Cesena FC family is thrilled to welcome manager Ashley Cole to lead the Bianconeri first team and extends a warm welcome to him and his staff in Cesena, wishing them all the best of luck in this new adventure."

‌



Although his deal initially runs until the end of the campaign, it can be extended 'upon the fulfilment of certain conditions'. Cole will be assisted by the former Chelsea academy coach Jack Mesure.

The 45-year-old's first game in the dugout could be as soon as Tuesday. Cesena will travel to face Mantova before hosting promotion-chasing Catanzaro next Saturday.

Will Ashley Cole be a successful manager? Give us your prediction in the comments section.

‌



Content cannot be displayed without consent

Cesena are hoping to gain promotion to Serie A but face a difficult task. The second division works on a play-off system, with the sides between fifth and eighth competing in an eliminator round before the winners join third and fourth in the semi-finals.

Fourth-placed Palermo currently sit 17 points clear of Cesena. Venezia and Monza occupy the two automatic promotion places.

‌



At the start of his coaching career in 2019, Cole opened up on his ambitions. "Everyone has their own philosophy but you also have to take ideas that you like from different coaches," he told Chelsea's website.

"The first thing I learned on my coaching badges was that every coach is a thief! I want to take parts of Jose Mourinho in terms of how organised he was, Carlo Ancelotti for his man-management and how he would give players faith to go out and express themselves within his system and Arsene Wenger, who was definitely a great man-manager and put a lot of trust in young players to find things out for themselves and learn quickly from mistakes. It's about being adaptable."

Football.title: Arne Slot singles out Liverpool star after Tottenham draw and responds to Anfield boos
Football.content:
Arne Slot singles out Liverpool star after Tottenham draw and responds to Anfield boos

Liverpool missed the chance to go fourth in the Premier League by drawing 1-1 at home to Tottenham, conceding a late equaliser which led to boos from the Anfield crowd

View 2 Images Arne Slot hailed one Liverpool star after the 1-1 draw with Spurs (Image: Gaspafotos/MB Media/Getty Images)

Arne Slot singled out Rio Ngumoha for praise after the Liverpool wonderkid impressed on his first Premier League start. But it wasn't to be a dream full debut for the 17-year-old forward, as the Reds slipped to a 1-1 draw with Tottenham.

‌



Richarlison's equaliser in the 90th minute cancelled out Dominik Szoboszlai's early free-kick to end Spurs' run of sixth defeats on the spin. Despite Liverpool failing to score from opening play, Ngumoha was a constant threat.

‌



In his 64 minutes, the ex-Chelsea prospect completed all seven of is dribbles and had seven touches in Tottenham's penalty area. Ngumoha started ahead of Mo Salah, who was named on the bench, likely with Wednesday's must-win Champions League second leg at home to Galatasaray in mind.

‌



"I have 12-13 players that are able to bring good performances," Slot told BBC Radio 5 Live post-match. "Rio is doing that well it makes sense to play him. It is a mix of playing the ones that are fit and using the quality that these players can bring."

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Jamie Carragher eviscerates Liverpool and questions players' character after Spurs slip up READ MORE :

The Liverpool manager also responded to boos from the home fans at full-time. "I think it's understandable for fans to be frustrated because it happened already so many times that they've seen the home team not picking up the points that they're expecting to, us conceding goals in the last minute," Slot replied.

‌



"Now it's up to us to bring that frustration to Wednesday and come up with a big performance. Because that we are all frustrated, that's completely clear.

"And it's now up to me and the players to take that frustration into Wednesday evening and show the fans the performance and the results they deserve, because they've been supportive throughout the whole season to us. Then if it happens so many times that they are frustrated in the end of a game or after the final whistle, that makes complete sense to me."

Should Rio Ngumoha keep his place? Have your say in the comments section.

‌



View 2 Images Rio Ngumoha was a silver lining for Liverpool against Spurs (Image: Gaspafotos/MB Media/Getty Images)

On the game itself, the Dutchman said: "In the large parts of the game that we were the better team, I think that is a moment where you have to score the second. I think we had moments to do so quite a lot and in the part of the game where it was more equal – the last 15 to 20 minutes – on both sides, it was waiting for one of the two teams to score.

"They created the chances all in a similar way, the four or five moments they had: long ball, picking up the second ball and then try to be a threat in moments like that. We also picked up a lot of those second balls where we were able to counter attack them.

Article continues below

"But from all these counter-attacks - all these four versus three, three versus two - all these moments that I felt with the quality players we have that we should be able to either get a bigger chance or when we have it, to score it, we didn't and then in the last minute we conceded the equaliser and then there's not a lot of time left to make up for it."

Football.title: Roy Keane sends message to Man Utd over Michael Carrick after Wayne Rooney gives clear verdict
Football.content:
Roy Keane sends message to Man Utd over Michael Carrick after Wayne Rooney gives clear verdict

Roy Keane remains unconvinced by Michael Carrick's claims for the Manchester United manager job despite the impressive run of form the interim head coach has overseen

View 2 Images Roy Keane has delivered a new verdict on Michael Carrick's future (Image: Getty Images)

Roy Keane has reinforced his view that Michael Carrick shouldn't become Manchester United's next permanent head coach. Carrick, 44, has performed admirably since his return to Old Trafford in an interim capacity in mid-January, securing seven wins and one draw from his nine games at the helm.

‌



This impressive spell has lifted United to third in the Premier League standings and positioned them favourably to clinch Champions League qualification for next season. However, despite the Reds enjoying a dramatic improvement in results since Carrick's arrival, Keane remains unconvinced he's the right candidate to lead the club on a full-time basis.

‌



When questioned whether he would hand Carrick the United manager's role permanently in the summer, Keane told Sky Sports: "No, I think there are better options out there. I think the games that he has come in, he has won football matches and done a very good job. He has simplified things, but there has been no jeopardy in those games.

‌



Man Utd chiefs issued clear transfer demand during crucial Aston Villa win READ MORE :

"They've had free weeks to prepare for those matches, it has been good timing for him, and he has done a very good job. I take my hat off to him.

"And if he does get the job in the summer, good luck to him. But I think there are far better options out there for the next manager of Man United.

"If Carrick had been mentioned three months ago as the next United manager you'd have been locked up. He wasn't even in the reckoning! But with time he has got the opportunity and he has done a very good job. I just think there are better options out there."

‌



Meanwhile, ex-United forward Rooney shared a different opinion to Keane. Indeed, the 40-year-old told BBC 5 Live that he would give Carrick the full-time gig at the end of the season. "100 per cent he should," Rooney replied, when questioned if Carrick should be awarded the permanent role.

View 2 Images Man United interim head coach Michael Carrick has impressed since taking over in January (Image: PA)

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



"I knew this was going happen with Michael Carrick, I know him very well, I know his character and personality," Rooney added. "It needed a calm head, but someone who knows the place and the players needed some love and he's give them that.

"We're seeing the players play with more quality, we're seeing them more together as a team and they look like a very strong team. So, for me, why would you change?

"You've got someone come in who's got I think the best win percentage of any Manchester United manager after that many games, so, for me, he has to get the job."

‌



Keane and Rooney were speaking after United further bolstered their Champions League ambitions with a well-deserved 3-1 victory against Aston Villa on Sunday. The Reds opened the scoring just after the interval through Casemiro but were pulled level when Ross Barkley unleashed a powerful effort past Senne Lammens. However, Carrick's team demonstrated resilience and regained the advantage as Matheus Cunha found the net before Benjamin Sesko sealed the triumph with a late goal.

"We can enjoy winning," Carrick said post-match. "It's a good feeling and something we want to keep getting used to. We are keeping a lid on it and understanding where we are and what it takes to win games like today. We are in a good position at the moment but still a lot to play for."

Former United midfielder Keane initially stated that he doesn't believe Carrick should become the club's next permanent head coach following his second match back in January. Speaking after a 3-2 victory at Arsenal, Keane commended the impact Carrick had made following consecutive wins but felt he isn't the "right man for the job".

‌



"Two great performances but anyone can win two games," Keane said at the time. "If United win every game until the end of the season, I still wouldn't be giving him the job. I still wouldn't be convinced he's the man for the job. Absolutely not. They need a bigger and better manager. The size of the club and the challenges they face over the next few years, you want a manager who you feel can get them winning league titles.

"Are we going to sit here and believe Michael Carrick can get United winning league titles? It's not enough for me. I don't believe he is the man to get them winning league titles."

United's next fixture sees them travel to Bournemouth on Friday evening. Victory at the Vitality Stadium would see them establish a six-point cushion over fourth-placed Aston Villa, move eight clear of Liverpool in fifth and extend their advantage to six points over sixth-placed Chelsea.

Football.title: Dominik Szoboszlai demands Liverpool team-mates 'wake up' in scathing Reds verdict
Football.content:
Dominik Szoboszlai demands Liverpool team-mates 'wake up' in scathing Reds verdict

Liverpool's hopes of playing Champions League football next season took a hit after Richarlison scored a late equaliser for relegation-threatened Tottenham at Anfield on Sunday

View 2 Images Dominik Szoboszlai sent a message to Liverpool's dressing room after the draw with SPurs (Image: Robbie Jay Barratt - AMA/Getty Images)

Liverpool star Dominik Szoboszlai has called on his team-mates to 'wake up' after sleepwalking into a 1-1 draw at home to Tottenham. Szoboszlai, 25, had put Liverpool ahead with only 19 minutes on the clock, but Arne Slot's side failed to build on their lead and were made to pay in the dying embers.

‌



Former Everton star Richarlison popped up with the late equaliser for Spurs, who earned a huge point and ended their run of six consecutive defeats in all competitions. The result leaves Liverpool in fifth place, having missed the chance to leapfrog Aston Villa in fourth.

‌



Although the Premier League is set to have five Champions League spots for next season, Liverpool remain at serious risk of missing out with eight games to go. Chelsea are only one point behind the Reds while Brentford could move within two points if they beat Wolves on Monday.

‌



Szoboszlai expressed his frustration in a post-match interview with Sky Sports, warning his team-mates that they could end up in the 'Conference League' next season. "A huge disappointment, I don't know what happened," Liverpool's goalscorer began.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Jamie Carragher eviscerates Liverpool and questions players' character after Spurs slip up READ MORE :

OPINION Andy Dunn Arne Slot's reaction to Richarlison goal speaks volumes as Liverpool failings resurface

‌



"In the last minute, again, I don't know how many times this season already. We have to wake up. I feel flat. We have to wake up because if we carry on like this, we should be happy with the Conference League. I don't know why this is happening, I honestly don't know.

"I think in the first-half we played very well, we controlled the whole game and they hardly created chances apart from one or two headers. Second half we just didn't so the same things. We will sit down together, and this is the most difficult time, but we have to stick together."

The Hungary international also commented on the reaction at Anfield upon the full-time whistle. "I didn't hear the boos, but we can understand them," Szoboszlai admitted.

‌



Will Liverpool qualify for the Champions League? Give us your prediction in the comments section.

View 2 Images Dominik Szoboszlai put Liverpool in front against Tottenham (Image: Michael Regan/Getty Images)

"We are not performing how we should. They should stand behind us because last season we became champions four games before the end everybody was happy.

‌



"Support us in a difficult time. There is still time to show how much we want it, we have to stick together and fight for the club."

Liverpool will return action on Wednesday, hosting Galatasaray for the second leg of their Champions League round-of-16 tie. Failure to overturn their 1-0 defeat in Istanbul will see the Reds miss out on another trophy and only have the FA Cup to play for when it comes to silverware this season.

Football.title: Former Man City star puts call out to English players to join Marcus Rashford in La Liga
Football.content:
Former Man City star puts call out to English players to join Marcus Rashford in La Liga

Marcus Rashford has been one of several English players to take La Liga by storm in recent seasons

View 2 Images Marcus Rashford has found his groove again since joining Barcelona (Image: Getty Images)

Espanyol winger Tyrhys Dolan has called on more young footballers to make the move from England to Spain and follow Marcus Rashford's footsteps. We’ve seen more and more English footballers look to break into first-team football by joining top European leagues over the past decade than ever before.

‌



Spain’s top division, La Liga, has recently welcomed world renowned England internationals Jude Bellingham, Trent Alexander-Arnold and Rashford, who have achieved varying degrees of success there.

‌



Another lesser known Englishman who decided to move to Spain is Dolan, who left Blackburn Rovers to join Espanyol on a free transfer last summer. The 24-year-old former Manchester City academy starlet, who came through the ranks with Cole Palmer, has had an impressive debut season for the Barcelona-based side.

‌



FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Dolan made 47 appearances for Blackburn last season but playing top flight football for Espanyol this term has provided a greater challenge, one he has fully risen to as his side looks to qualify for European football this season. And he hopes others can follow the example set by himself, Bellingham and Rashford to benefit their own careers.

When asked whether he thinks other English youngsters should join La Liga, Dolan said: "Definitely. Without a doubt. I'd say if you've got the opportunity to play in the top leagues abroad, then you should go for it.

‌



Barcelona draw up Marcus Rashford back-up plan as two transfers eyed in case deal collapses READ MORE :

"I think you've seen what it’s like. It's obviously not easy. Not everyone who has come from England to play in La Liga has hit the ground running but I just think what it can do for your career is amazing.

"Just in terms of learning and adding more tools to your trade. I think, for me, I've learned so much in maybe eight or nine months. I feel like I've learnt a lot football-wise and about myself. So yeah, without a doubt, I'd definitely say it's [worth doing] if you’ve got the opportunity to take it."

View 2 Images Tyrhys Dolan signed for Espanyol last summer (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Sky Sports discounted Premier League and EFL package This article contains affiliate links, we will receive a commission on any sales we generate from it. Learn more £43 £35 Sky Get the deal here Product Description Sky has slashed the price of its Essential TV and Sky Sports bundle ahead of the 2025/26 season, saving members £192 and offering more than 1,400 live matches across the Premier League, EFL and more. Sky will show at least 215 live Premier League games next season, an increase of up to 100 more.

He has managed four assists and a goal in 26 La Liga appearances this season. Meanwhile, Rashford has also enjoyed a stellar run of form, managing 13 goals and ten assists across all competitions, with Barcelona expected to make his loan deal permanent this summer.

Dolan went on to explain how adapting to a new league has helped improve his own game.

‌



He said: "For me, it's little things like body positioning in terms of if players are attacking. I know I'm an attacker but that's a defensive role because we're such a hardworking team and just the positions I'm taking up on the pitch, like where to be when the ball's in a certain area, how to produce more of an end product and get more crosses in.

"There's been so many things because, when you step up a level, naturally you need to raise your game in all areas and the staff have been great working one-to-one with me and a lot of off-pitch conversations to do that.

"So yeah, there's a lot of aspects to the game and I feel like I'm understanding the game a lot more now. Hopefully I can keep doing more of that and the successes will come with it."

Football.title: Jamie Carragher eviscerates Liverpool and questions players' character after Spurs slip up
Football.content:
Jamie Carragher eviscerates Liverpool and questions players' character after Spurs slip up

Jamie Carragher didn't hold back when speaking about Liverpool's players following the 1-1 Premier League draw with Tottenham Hotspur at Anfield

View 2 Images Jamie Carragher took aim at Liverpool's players following the draw with Tottenham Hotspur

Jamie Carragher told Liverpool’s players they don’t have it in them to dig in and make it difficult for the opposition after they slipped up against Tottenham. What has fast turned into a poor campaign for the Reds took another turn for the worse against Igor Tudor’s side.

‌



Despite taking the lead through Dominik Szoboszlai’s fine free-kick after just 18 minutes, Richarlison struck a minute from time to secure his side a precious point in their battle to avoid the drop. And Carragher, speaking in the immediate aftermath of the full-time whistle going, couldn’t help but take aim at Liverpool’s players.

‌



He said: "It was an awful performance. No disrespect to Tottenham, but they are the worst team in the Premier League now, probably for the last couple of months.

‌



“They are exactly who you want to play, playing at home, the incentive of your results this weekend, and to put in a performance like that. But it's been like that all season.

"And the big question on everybody's lips is that down to the manager or the makeup of the squad? Could a new manager completely revitalise these type of players and bring that energy and sort of intensity back into this team? I'm not sure.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Richarlison stuns Liverpool with late goal as Tottenham earn lifeline - 5 talking points READ MORE :

Igor Tudor makes embarrassing error before Liverpool match as Arne Slot mix-up captured READ MORE :

"I think a lot of it goes back to what happened in the summer, the profile of the players that came in, and there's just too many players who just want to play when the ball's at their feet.

“They don't want to dig in and make it difficult. That's why it's so easy to play against this Liverpool team."

And speaking after Richarlison’s equaliser, Carragher claimed: "Well it was coming. I've been saying all second-half, Liverpool have been sleepwalking into this result.

‌



“And it was always going to be him, he's looked the most dangerous Tottenham player. And Liverpool just don't challenge for the long ball.

View 2 Images Spurs star Richarlison celebrates after scoring against Liverpool (Image: Michael Regan/Getty Images)

“Andy Robertson doesn't challenge for the long hopeful ball. Does the jump, gets in-between, not sure where Van Dijk's going - doesn't react quickly enough and then Liverpool are all over the place.

‌



“But I can't say Tottenham don't deserve it, they've had chances in the game. The majority fell to this man, but this was definitely their best one.

“Van Dijk is furious, but this has been coming...so obvious. As I said, you can't say they don't deserve it."

Football.title: Igor Tudor in tense Sky Sports interview after Liverpool draw as Spurs boss hits out at question
Football.content:
Igor Tudor in tense Sky Sports interview after Liverpool draw as Spurs boss hits out at question

Tottenham Hotspur head coach Igor Tudor engaged in a back-and-forth with Sky Sports reporter Patrick Davidson about being asked again about his future with Spurs

View 2 Images Igor Tudor's interviews continue to be appointment viewing

Igor Tudor called out Sky Sports reporter Patrick Davidson during a live interview after being asked about his Tottenham Hotspur future. The interim Spurs boss insisted that head coaches should not answer such questions and that they should instead be put to the club, as they make the decisions.

‌



Tudor spoke after Tottenham drew 1-1 with Liverpool at Anfield. Richarlison scored a late equaliser for the visitors to cancel out a free-kick from Dominik Szoboszlai that opened the scoring on Sunday.

‌



After losing his first five matches in charge, Tudor's team finally picked up a point. However, there are still questions about his future on the sideline.

‌



Igor Tudor makes embarrassing error before Liverpool match as Arne Slot mix-up captured READ MORE :

Jamie Carragher eviscerates Liverpool and questions players' character after Spurs slip up READ MORE :

Davidson asked: "Have you shown that you can provide that hope? That you don't need a new manager?"

Tudor responded by appearing to put his hand on the interviewer's shoulder before interrupting his question. The Croatian interjected: "It's enough, I always tell you what I think."

The reporter responded: "But do you not think sometimes these questions need to be asked and need to be answered? You've got a lot of Spurs fans all around the world..."

‌



Having heard enough, Tudor replied: "That's not a question for me, I'm the coach. You need to ask me about how we play." Davidson insisted: "I have," with Tudor agreeing: "Yeah, and I answered.

View 2 Images Igor Tudor has lost five of his six matches as Tottenham Hotspur head coach (Image: Getty Images)

"This question journalists like to ask is a question that doesn't have sense, when you ask the coach about their position, this doesn't make any sense. All the coaches are always telling you the same things, but you always ask this.

‌



"So the coach needs to answer something because he's obliged to come on this press conference; otherwise, he would like to stay home, but you always insist with almost the same questions."

Davidson explained: "No, but the point being, you can provide the hope, right? That's something you said in an interview on Friday," before being cut off again.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



Tudor claimed: "I didn't say that. What did I say?" The reporter reiterated: "You said people need hope of a new manager sometimes, and my point is only, have you shown today with the way Tottenham play that you can provide hope?"

The Spurs boss asked in response: "What do you think I will answer now? What I will answer, do you probably hope? What kind of questions? What do I need to tell you now? No?"

Davidson clarified: "You can say whatever you like." Tudor continued: "I tell you this, what will I tell my players? Of course, so this is the answer you want to hear."

Article continues below

The reporter then repeated: "You can just say whatever you like." Tudor then looked away, before asking: "Did we finish, or no?" To which Davidson agreed: "We're finished."

Football.title: Arne Slot's reaction to Richarlison goal speaks volumes as Liverpool failings resurface
Football.content:
Arne Slot's reaction to Richarlison goal speaks volumes as Liverpool failings resurface

LIVERPOOL 1-1 TOTTENHAM: Arne Slot's side sleepwalked into a 1-1 draw with relegation-battling Tottenham, missing the chance to go fourth in the Premier League table

View 2 Images Arne Slot and Igor Tudor felt contrasting emotions (Image: Carl Recine/Getty Images)

One has a single point from his four Premier League matches, one won the Premier League title in his first season. But Arne Slot looked like the manager under pressure when Richarlison mis-hit a scruffy equaliser as the Anfield clock ticked towards added time.

‌



Igor Tudor, whose calamitous spell as Spurs manager could still end soon, leapt into and punched the air, while Slot could only shake his head in disbelief. This result was a significant blow for Slot and Liverpool but a bigger issue for the Dutchman is the growing discontent around Anfield.

‌



The nature of the setback - Liverpool yet again conceding a late goal - was certain to prompt some jeering but there was not a great amount of enthusiasm for the home performance ahead of Richarlison's leveller. In fact, Liverpool had only been ahead through a goalkeeping error.

‌



Dominik Szoboszlai has established himself as one of the Premier League's most lethal set-piece exponents and his first half strike was typically sweet. It was firmly hit and had a smidgeon of sideways movement but Guglielmo Vicario really should have saved it. In fact, he probably should have shuffled across and caught it.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Richarlison stuns Liverpool with late goal as Tottenham earn lifeline - 5 talking points READ MORE :

Igor Tudor makes embarrassing error before Liverpool match as Arne Slot mix-up captured READ MORE :

Perhaps Tudor has managed to undermine the confidence of two 'keepers, having made the ill-fated decision not to start Vicario against Atletico Madrid. Under Tudor, Spurs have maintained their knack of conceding soft goals. In fact, they might just have upped it.

‌



Not that Szoboszlai should have held back on his celebrations. This was his fourth goal from direct free-kicks this season and his satisfaction was probably enhanced by a spot of frustration at again being deployed at right-back. Of course he performs his designated duty with diligence - and no little excellence - but it is obvious he does not relish the role.

He was there because Slot was using Jeremie Frimpong in the Mo Salah position, with Rio Ngumoha on the left flank. Such has been Salah’s extended dip in form, Slot’s decision was no headline-grabber, although you suspect the Egyptian will return for Champions League duty in midweek.

Ngumoha's contribution was exciting, if a little inconsistent. He is a raw talent, that is for sure. And the 17-year-old stood out in a Liverpool team that is still groping for its best form.

‌



Will Liverpool qualify for the Champions League? Give us your prediction in the comments section.

View 2 Images Richarlison snatched a draw in the 90th minute (Image: Darren Staples/AFP via Getty Images)

Not everything he did came off. Occasionally, he chose the wrong option. He was slightly off-cue when trying to convert a couple of half-chances. But Anfield still sang his name, still acclaimed a 17-year-old who will shine on this stage for many years to come.

‌



Ngumoha did not play in the Egyptian's position but he started and Salah did not. It felt like a significant moment in Liverpool time. Salah was sent on for half an hour and looked a certainty to score on the counter-attack on more than one occasion.

But in a microcosm of his season, his finishing was panicky. He has gone from instinctive to indecisive. Ultimately, Salah's failure to put the outcome beyond doubt proved costly as Andy Robertson failed to make a relatively simple clearance and Randal Kolo Muani set up Richarlison's equaliser.

This season, Liverpool have now conceded eight Premier League goals in the 90th minute or later and they have dropped seven points from winning positions at Anfield. No wonder the locals jeered.

Football.title: Nijman beats Price in final to secure European Darts Trophy
Football.content:
Wessel Nijman was crowned the winner of the Elten Safety Shoes European Darts Trophy with a history-making win over Gerwyn Price in Germany.

The Dutchman, 25, beat a trio of Premier League stars including Josh Rock and Gian van Veen on Sunday to scoop the £35,000 top prize and catapult himself into the world's top 20.

He also remarkably registered five consecutive ton-plus averages in Göttingen, including in the 8-3 victory over Price in the final.

After beating Mike De Decker on Saturday, Nijman began Sunday's action with a 6-1 takedown of Van Veen, averaging 112.87, his highest on the big stage.

He took this form into the quarter-finals, beating Rock 6-2 and averaging 106 before blowing away fellow countryman Niels Zonneveld 7-1 in the semi-final with a 110.41 average.

That set up a final showdown with Price, who was gunning to become the second player to win 10 European titles after Michael van Gerwen, who suffered a shock exit in the earlier rounds.

But Nijman overpowered his opponent, with a brilliant 11-darter along the way.

'There are no words to describe how I'm feeling'

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Referee Huw Ware was not pleased with the crowd, earlier in the tournament, during round two of the PDC European Darts Trophy match between Stephen Bunting and Niko Springer

Following the win, Nijman, who averaged 100.8 against Price, said: "I think there are no words to describe how I'm feeling right now."

"I felt so great all day long. I think you saw when I played Gian, Josh and Niels, I played so well.

"I thought to myself: 'This could be the day you win a title', and I'm so happy I did."

Image: Nijman celebrates with the trophy (Pic: Paul Targyik/PDC Europe)

He added: "I didn't need to prove anything to myself. I needed to prove to other people that I can play like this on the big stage.

"I'm doing it now in a European Tour event and I hope I can do it in the biggest major events, so I can show the world that I'm capable of winning any event there is.

"The main goal this year was to get into the top 16 and be a seeded player in major tournaments, and I think this is a big step towards that."

World champion Luke Littler was absent from the European Darts Trophy having previously hit out at the hostile receptions he has received in Germany.

Following his defeat on Saturday to Niko Springer, Stephen Bunting said he was left "really hurt" by the booing, which even led to referee Huw Ware telling the crowd to "shut up".

European Darts Trophy: Sunday's results

Image: Nijman was in imperious form throughout the tournament (pic: Paul Targyik/PDC Europe)

Final

Wessel Nijman 8-3 Gerwyn Price

Semi-finals

Football.title: Richarlison stuns Anfield to earn precious point for Spurs
Football.content:
Richarlison scored a 90th-minute equaliser as Tottenham stunned Anfield to draw 1-1 with Liverpool as Igor Tudor finally stopped the rot in dramatic fashion.

When Guglielmo Vicario, the goalkeeper recalled after Antonin Kinsky's nightmare in Spain, contrived to let in Dominik Szoboszlai's first-half free-kick, Spurs were facing a seventh straight defeat in all competitions - and a fifth since Tudor took over.

But Arne Slot's side failed to press home their advantage in the second half, Richarlison having four chances before he finally did level it in the final minute of the 90. The result means Spurs are one point above the drop zone. Liverpool are fifth, below Aston Villa.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Spurs forward Richarlison fires home equaliser in 90th-minute against Liverpool

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Dominik Szoboszlai puts Liverpool ahead as Jamie Carragher has harsh words for Guglielmo Vicario

Player ratings: Liverpool: Alisson (5), Szoboszlai (7), Gomez (6), Van Dijk (6), Robertson (5), Gravenberch (6), Mac Allister (5), Frimpong (7), Ngumoha (8), Wirtz (7), Gakpo (7).



Subs: Salah (7), Ekitike (6), Jones (5), Nyoni (6), Chiesa (n/a).



Tottenham: Vicario (6), Porro (7), Danso (6), Dragusin (5); Spence (7), Sarr (7), Gray (7), Souza (6), Richarlison (8), Solanke (6), Tel (7).



Subs: Simons (6), Kolo Muani (7), Olusesi (6), Rowswell (n/a).



Player of the Match: Richarlison.

Tudor's team show some fight

There was an expectation that Tudor's time was up when Tottenham capitulated against Atletico Madrid in midweek but this was a spirited showing from a depleted side that few had fancied could possibly come up with anything at Anfield. Spurs showed fight.

Without over a dozen first-team players, Tottenham had seen relegation rivals West Ham, Leeds and Nottingham Forest all pick up draws to leave them outside of the relegation zone only by goal difference at kick-off. The pressure was on but they battled.

The home game against Forest next week remains massive for the prospects of retaining their Premier League status but for the first time in a long time there is a little evidence that Spurs are prepared to scrap to stay in this. Supporters will take that right now.

Team news headlines Rio Ngumoha came into the Liverpool team for his first Premier League start. Alisson Becker was among four others to return as Mo Salah and Hugo Ekitike were benched.



Radu Dragusin and Souza came in with Cristian Romero and Micky van de Ven unavailable. Igor Tudor named only seven Tottenham substitutes.

Tudor: Team spirit was amazing

Tottenham head coach Igor Tudor speaking to Sky Sports:

"We showed the spirit, the team spirit.

"This moment is really strange but I said to the players it is even more challenging to show who we are as players, as a team. At this moment, it is really difficult to work on any kind of structure in attack, so we had to simplify it.

"The team spirit was amazing, they were very honest to give everything they had in this particular moment. This was the demand. And then the quality is there.

"Every point is crucial, every point counts. We go game by game."

What went wrong for Liverpool?

Szoboszlai's fourth free-kick goal of the Premier League season, a Liverpool record, had put Slot's side in position to bury an opponent low on confidence. Rio Ngumoha was certainly in the mood on his first Premier League start, but Liverpool did not finish it.

Richarlison had warned them, scoring with his fifth shot of the game - more than any other player. Randal Kolo Muani outmuscled Andy Robertson and Virgil van Dijk to set up the chance, another damning reflection of Liverpool's defending.

They allowed the opposition back in and paid the price, a feature of their title defence. Liverpool had lost their previous game in the competition against bottom club Wolves but this was in front of their own fans, who booed at the full-time whistle.

Awful by Liverpool, says Carragher

Sky Sports' Jamie Carragher at Anfield:

"It was an awful performance. No disrespect to Tottenham but they are the worst team in the Premier League now, probably for the last couple of months. They are exactly who you want to play, playing at home, the incentive of your results this weekend and to put in performance like that. But it's been like that all season.

"And the big question on everybody's lips - is that down to the manager or the makeup of the squad? Could a new manager completely revitalise these type of players and bring that energy and sort of intensity back into this team? I'm not sure.

"I think a lot of it goes back to what happened in the summer, the profile of the players that came in, and there's just too many players who just want to play when the ball's at their feet. They don't want to dig in and make it difficult. That's why it's so easy to play against this Liverpool team."

Story of the match in stats

Football.title: Carragher slams Liverpool display against Spurs as hopes of CL spot hit
Football.content:
Jamie Carragher has branded Liverpool's 1-1 draw with Tottenham as a "disaster" and castigated the performance of Arne Slot's misfiring side.

Richarlison's late leveller grabbed a vital point for Tottenham in their fight against relegation but dealt a heavy blow to Liverpool's Champions League qualification hopes.

Liverpool are fifth, a point above Chelsea and two behind Aston Villa.

"This is an absolute disaster for Liverpool in terms of their Champions League qualification," said Carragher.

"They have actually made Tottenham look like a decent team."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Spurs forward Richarlison fired home the equaliser in 90th the minute against Liverpool

Ex-Everton striker Richarlison had squandered a number of chances before finding the net in front of the Kop in the 90th minute.

Liverpool hit by another stoppage-time goal Liverpool have now conceded eight Premier League goals in the 90th minute or later this season - their most in a single campaign.

"The defending was absolutely shocking from Liverpool. I've been saying all half that Liverpool have been sleepwalking into this.

Liverpool drop crucial points again Liverpool have dropped seven points from winning positions at Anfield in the Premier League this season, their most since the 2016-17 campaign (also seven).

"It was an awful performance. No disrespect to Tottenham but they are the worst team in the Premier League now, probably for the last couple of months. They are exactly who you want to play, playing at home, the incentive of your results this weekend and to put in a performance like that. But it's been like that all season.

"And the big question on everybody's lips is: 'Is that down to the manager or the make-up of the squad?' Could a new manager completely revitalise these type of players and bring that energy and sort of intensity back into this team? I'm not sure.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Dominik Szoboszlai puts Liverpool ahead from a free-kick but Jamie Carragher had some harsh words for the Spurs goalkeeper Guglielmo Vicario.

"I think a lot of it goes back to what happened in the summer, the profile of the players that came in and there's just too many players who just want to play when the ball's at their feet. They don't want to dig in and make it difficult. That's why it's so easy to play against this Liverpool team."

Szoboszlai: We must wake up or settle for Conference League

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Speaking after the 1-1 draw with Tottenham in the Premier League, Dominik Szoboszlai suggested that Liverpool's performance levels need to improve otherwise they'll be forced to settle for Conference League football next season.

Dominik Szoboszlai says Liverpool should be happy to qualify for the Conference League if they continue to play like they did in their draw with Tottenham.

"We have to wake up. If we keep going like this, next season we should be happy if we play Conference League," Szoboszlai told Sky Sports.

"In the first half, we played very well. We controlled the whole game, they hardly created many chances.

"Second half, I don't know, we just don't do the same things like in the first half. The question is why? We will sit down together. This is the most difficult time. We have to stick together.

"I didn't hear the boos [at full-time] but I can understand because last season we became champions and this season we are not performing in the way that we should be.

"They should stay behind us because last season, when we were champions four games before the end, everybody was happy. Support us now when we're in difficult times."

Slot on familiar failings costing them vs Spurs

Liverpool head coach Arne Slot aired his frustration at the familiar way his side dropped points against Spurs.

He was keen to flag some positive aspects to his team's performance but ultimately rued their failure to score more of their chances on Sunday.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Tottenham boss Igor Tudor appeared to mistake Spurs' player liaison officer Allan Dixon for Liverpool head coach Arne Slot ahead of their Premier League fixture at Anfield

"Every time when it happens it feels like it's the biggest blow. When it happened against Wolves, it felt like it was the biggest blow of the season and now it happens again.

"Again, in the last minute, we get an opportunity to score afterwards ourselves, which we didn't do, and then we leave the pitch with a disappointing result.

"The way we concede is not always the same in the last minute, so you can't compare the deflected shot against Wolves with the one we conceded today, or the 30-yard shot against Fulham in the top corner, so it's different in the way we concede, but the same thing is that we constantly - or mainly - if we drop points, concede goals either in extra time or as today in the last minute.

"It's clear that there were a few things positive today.

"If you look at the great goal we scored with Dominik, if you look at the performance of Rio [Ngumoha] for a 17-year-old, if you look at the chances we've created... But we are never able to outscore the amount of chances we have, so you always expect more goals than we are creating.

"But it's also completely clear that there are parts of our game where we definitely need to improve, and that's also why we are in this position, because not everything, actually, a lot of things are not perfect. And as a result of that, we're not able to win or to get as many points as we want to."

To put Slot's comment about chance conversion in context, in the Premier League this season Liverpool have scored 49 times from a total Expected Goals (xG) figure of 50.01.

So Liverpool have performed almost exactly to their xG. Ten teams have a higher underperformance.

In fact, across the last eight Premier League games, Liverpool have scored 16 goals from an xG of just 14.3.

Against Spurs, they had an xG of 1.67 compared to the one goal they did score.

Football.title: Thrilling Six Nations leaves questions for England, Scotland, Ireland, Wales
Football.content:
It is being touted as the most entertaining Six Nations ever, and looking at the pre-tournament script compared to the end credits, you can see just why.

'Unpredictability' was the defining characteristic of a Six Nations showing that left the audience gripped at every twist and turn.

It was a non-stop action blockbuster with a finale that lived up to the hype as a total of 29 tries were scored across 'Super Saturday', the most ever for a single day in the championship's history and two more than the previous highest in 2015.

Image: France are Six Nations champions once again

Six teams, six potential protagonists gave their all, and while they now lick their wounds and prepare in anticipation of another battle in the near future, the dissection gets under way of just how and why France were the heroes of the tale once again.

It is a series that begs the questions of why England could not live up to their hype, why Scotland can be in the race but never win it, if Italy have the potential for 'leading man' status, if Wales can be restored to their former glory, and if Ireland's time in the spotlight is coming to a close.

A walking contradiction: France's attacking nous the key
