Sports.title: Michael Carrick answers question on possible Casemiro U-turn after eye-catching display
Sports.content:
Michael Carrick answers question on possible Casemiro U-turn after eye-catching display

Manchester United fans made their feelings on Casemiro clear during the Red Devils' 3-1 win over Aston Villa on Sunday, as he enters the final eight matches of his tenure at Old Trafford

View 2 Images Michael Carrick has spoken about Casemiro after his goal for Manchester United against Aston Villa (Image: Catherine Ivill - AMA, Getty Images)

Michael Carrick still believes that Casemiro will leave Manchester United this summer, despite suggestions that the club could try to convince him to stay. Both parties have agreed that the 34-year-old will leave the club when his contract expires at the end of this season.

‌



However, his recent performances have been outstanding, as evidenced by him playing a part in all three goals as United thumped Aston Villa 3-1 on Sunday. Casemiro netted the first goal and then provided crucial passes leading up to goals for Matheus Cunha and Benjamin Sesko, bolstering United's hold on a Champions League spot for 2026/27.

‌



The ex-Real Madrid midfielder has featured in all nine matches under Carrick's management so far and is showcasing some of the finest football of his four-year tenure at Old Trafford. However, he earns £350,000-a-week, and his departure this summer will aid in trimming United's wage bill.

‌



Man Utd chiefs issued clear transfer demand during crucial Aston Villa win READ MORE :

But during Sunday's victory, the Stretford End chanted 'one more year, one more year Casemiro', and Carrick was questioned whether a reversal could be made before the season's end. He replied: "I think in some ways it's difficult to say, I think it's when something's decided and in some ways the fact that it was decided makes things a little bit easier and everyone understands the situation really.

"I think the impact he's had has been terrific, certainly since I've been here and working with him and his influence within the team and big moments and goals. It was a nice moment at the end there with the supporters and having that connection, and the respect."

United could save upwards of £18million annually in wages when Casemiro leaves, but finding a replacement will be an enormous challenge. He's netted seven goals in the Premier League this campaign and has rediscovered his form to become one of the division's outstanding holding midfielders.

‌



FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

As a five-time Champions League victor with Real Madrid, he also brings considerable experience to the club, though Carrick dismissed suggestions that replacing him would be a near-impossible undertaking. He added: "I think, this is totally no disrespect to Case, he's been fantastic, he has been a big player for us and has been important in the dressing room and one that I have spoken to and connected with really well.

"But I think as a club and as a team, your players come and go, some may be bigger, some may be more important than others at different times.

‌



View 2 Images Casemiro will leave Man United this summer (Image: OLI SCARFF, AFP via Getty Images)

"I don't think it's ever really about replacing them like for like, I think you can go with different directions, you understand what the balance of the squad will need, whether that's on the pitch, off the pitch leadership, positionally, there's all sorts of different things going into it.

"Case's done some really, really good things and certainly since I've been here he's been an absolute pleasure to work with."

Sports.title: Richarlison stuns Liverpool with late goal as Tottenham earn lifeline - 5 talking points
Sports.content:
Richarlison stuns Liverpool with late goal as Tottenham earn lifeline - 5 talking points

LIVERPOOL 1-1 TOTTENHAM: Ex-Everton star Richarlison was the hero for Spurs, handing Igor Tudor a lifeline and giving his team a much-needed boost in their fight for Premier League survival

View 5 Images Richarlison scored a 90th-minute equaliser (Image: Michael Regan/Getty Images)

Pantomime villain Richarlison rescued a precious point for Tottenham after Liverpool sleepwalked into a 1-1 draw. The former Everton star netted a goal in the 90th minute to silence the Kop End and boost Spurs' survival hopes.

‌



It'd looked like the end for Tottenham boss Igor Tudor, who was on the cusp of his fifth defeat in as many games. Dominik Szoboszlai had given Liverpool an early lead and the Reds were on course to jump into fourth place.

‌



Manchester United had done Arne Slot's side a favour by beating Aston Villa 3-1 earlier on Sunday, but the reigning champions couldn't take advantage. Instead, Spurs managed to match fellow strugglers West Ham and Nottingham Forest's results with a draw of their own.

‌



Here are five talking points from Anfield.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Igor Tudor appears to make embarrassing Arne Slot gaffe ahead of Liverpool clash READ MORE :

Liverpool activate Alisson contract option as former boss eyes reunion READ MORE :

1. Slot rings the changes

Trailing in their Champions League last-16 tie with Galatasaray, some rotation was on the cards for Liverpool. But few at Anfield expected both Hugo Ekitike and Mo Salah to come out of the starting XI.

‌



Rio Ngumoha was handed his first Premier League start on the left wing while Cody Gakpo played as a striker. Jamie Carragher reacted to the team news by saying: "A little bit of a shock because Arne Slot normally likes to keep a settled team, and he's done that for the last few weeks, especially after Mo Salah got back from AFCON.

"There's a big game in midweek, so he [Slot] obviously sees this as a game that he can afford to make changes in."

Will Tottenham get relegated? Give us your prediction in the comments section.

‌



View 5 Images Rio Ngumoha was handed his first Premier League start (Image: Liverpool FC/Liverpool FC via Getty Images)

2. Szoboszlai makes Liverpool history

Some things don't change. Like Szoboszlai scoring free-kicks and Tottenham having a poor goalkeeper.

When Archie Gray fouled Alexis Mac Allister around 20 yards from goal, many in the Kop End would've put money on the ball hitting the back of the net. Indeed it did, although Szboszlai didn't need to produce one of his best to break the deadlock - more on that later.

‌



The Reds' makeshift right-back has now scored four direct free-kicks in the Premier League this season - the most ever by a Liverpool player in a single campaign in the competition.

View 5 Images Dominik Szoboszlai is a certified free-kick specialist (Image: Liverpool FC/Liverpool FC via Getty Images)

3. Vicario gives Tottenham deja vu

Back in goal after his 17-minute spell on the bench in midweek, Guglielmo Vicario had a point to prove. On his first assignment, the Italian failed.

‌



Szoboszlai's free-kick was palmed in by Vicario, and Tottenham's defenders must've had a serious case of deja vu after Antonin Kinsky's nightmare against Atletico Madrid. Whether it be poor footwork or weak hands, Vicario was fault and gave Spurs an even bigger challenge.

View 5 Images Guglielmo Vicario's form has plummeted this season (Image: Robbie Jay Barratt - AMA/Getty Images)

4. Richarlison rages after Van Dijk battle

A familiar foe in these parts, Richarlison was always going to find himself in a contentious situation. The Brazilian scored the all-important equaliser but was involved in an incident beforehand.

‌



After Dominic Solanke brushed off Joe Gomez, Richarlison got himself in a foot race with Virgil van Dijk. The Liverpool captain was grappling with the Tottenham forward, who went bundling over in the penalty area.

Richarlison was adamant that Spurs should've been awarded a penalty, claiming that he'd had his shirt pulled, but referee Chris Kavanagh wasn't interested. Nor was VAR, as Spurs continued to toil in their pursuit of an equaliser.

View 5 Images Richarlison believed that Tottenham should've had a penalty (Image: Darren Staples/AFP via Getty Images)

‌



5. Who else but him?

Proudly playing his part as Anfield's pantomime villain, of course Richarlison would have another say. With Liverpool failing to put the game to bed and leaving the back door open, Randal Kolo Muani held off Van Dijk inside the penalty area to give Richarlison a simple finish.

The Tottenham hero was almost left red-faced when Liverpool broke away in stoppage time, only for Ekitike to miss a golden opportunity. Boos rang around Anfield on the full-time whistle.

Pure drama.

Sports.title: Bryan Mbeumo 'shakes head' at Man Utd team-mate as Gary Neville raises concern
Sports.content:
Bryan Mbeumo 'shakes head' at Man Utd team-mate as Gary Neville raises concern

Bryan Mbeumo went off during Manchester United's 3-1 win over Aston Villa, with the forward seen shaking his head at team-mate Diogo Dalot when leaving the pitch

View 2 Images Bryan Mbeumo sat down on the Old Trafford pitch before being substituted (Image: OLI SCARFF, AFP via Getty Images)

Gary Neville expressed concerns over a potential injury to Bryan Mbeumo after seeing him 'shaking his head' and speaking to Diogo Dalot following his substitution during Manchester United's 3-1 victory over Aston Villa at Old Trafford. Benjamin Sesko was brought on as a replacement for the 26-year-old and netted United's third goal of the match as they continued their fine form.

‌



Mbeumo has gone off in each of United's previous three games. However, he was hobbling on this occasion while walking off the pitch.

‌



The forward also appeared to exchange words with Dalot as he exited the pitch. Neville picked up on this interaction with his team-mate whilst working as a co-commentator for the match on Sky Sports.

‌



Man Utd have plan to sell five stars as brutal £422m transfer truth laid bare READ MORE :

Neville said: "Is he injured? Is he limping, Mbeumo? He was shaking his head when he walked off and looked at one of the United players as if he might be carrying something."

Mbeumo's body language will no doubt be a bit of a worry for United and Carrick. The forward now hasn't scored for United in his last five Premier League matches, but has arguably been their standout performer this season.

United were leading 2-1 at that point, with Mbeumo replaced five minutes after Matheus Cunha had restored their advantage. Casemiro had given the hosts an early second-half lead before Ross Barkley levelled for Villa in controversial circumstances, with Amadou Onana narrowly avoiding a VAR decision against him.

‌



Sesko then added United's third and final goal within five minutes of coming on for Mbeumo. They maintained control of the game from that point, with Manuel Ugarte replacing Casemiro for the final stages.

View 2 Images Gary Neville spotted Bryan Mbeumo shaking his head (Image: Lewis Storey, Getty Images)

Mbeumo has featured in every fixture under Michael Carrick, with the opening match he oversaw coinciding with the Cameroon international's return from the Africa Cup of Nations. Nevertheless, he went off in all but one of those encounters.

‌



Mbeumo eventually completed the full 90 minutes away to West Ham United, setting up Sesko's 96th-minute leveller. The duo also linked up once more for the solitary strike in United's 1-0 victory over Everton before he limped off seven minutes afterwards, being replaced by Noussair Mazraoui.

It prompted uncertainty about his availability against Crystal Palace, but he started and played 85 minutes before Zirkzee entered the fray again. Carrick also made the identical substitution in their 2-1 loss to Newcastle United.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Their next fixture is on Friday evening, with a shorter-than-usual gap before next week's journey to Bournemouth. Mbeumo appears to be an early concern for that one.

However, that represents their final match before the international break, meaning United do not play again until 13 April. Consequently, if Mbeumo does miss out on the south coast, there is a fortnight for him to recuperate before Carrick has to rely on him again.

Sunday's triumph, meanwhile, saw United strengthen their hold on a Champions League spot. Carrick's side now sit comfortably in third place and crucially, three points above fifth position.

Article continues below

Sports.title: Michael Carrick makes Bruno Fernandes appeal after record-breaking Man Utd display
Sports.content:
Michael Carrick makes Bruno Fernandes appeal after record-breaking Man Utd display

Manchester United beat Aston Villa at Old Trafford on Sunday to bolster their hopes of a Champions League spot with captain Bruno Fernandes playing a starring role once again

View 2 Images Bruno Fernandes' future at Man United remains up in the air (Image: Getty Images)

Michael Carrick hailed Bruno Fernandes after seeing him break a record belonging to David Beckham in Manchester United's crucial win over Aston Villa.

‌



'Captain Fantastic' Fernandes set-up two of United's three goals in the 3-1 triumph at Old Trafford. The win saw United cement their place in third - and take a huge step towards Champions League qualification.

‌



And in the process, Fernandes reached 100 assists in all competitions in the Premier League era, while overtaking Beckham's United record of 15 in a single season, set in 2000. The future of Fernandes remains unclear, with the Portuguese star admitting he will wait until the summer before deciding his next move.

‌



But Carrick outlined Fernandes's ongoing value to the team when he said: "We're delighted to have him. He's not someone you'd want to lose.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Man Utd get immediate Aston Villa goal explanation as Gary Neville spots telling moment READ MORE :

"He's proven over a period of time how impactful he is in the big moments. He's there all the time.

‌



"You can always count on him to be there. It's a really good trait to have. He's been through ups and downs here but it's never knocked him. He is there to make the difference."

On his milestone, Fernandes said: "I am more pleased because I did it serving my team-mates. That is even better, giving joy to the others is very good. I am very happy I can help them to score and be happy. It's a huge achievement for me."

View 2 Images Fernandes shone in Man United's win vs Aston Villa (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Villa have now lost three league games on the spin and boss Unai Emery said: "We could have dominated more. We lost it in the second half.

"We have been struggling more in the last two months. We competed, but United played a very intelligent match. But we can get better. This s a bad result for us now, but we must prepare for the next challenge."

Sports.title: Igor Tudor makes embarrassing error before Liverpool match as Arne Slot mix-up captured
Sports.content:
Igor Tudor makes embarrassing error before Liverpool match as Arne Slot mix-up captured

Liverpool played host to Tottenham Hotspur at Anfield on Sunday afternoon, and Igor Tudor was spotted on live TV making quite the gaffe after making his way down the tunnel prior to kick-off

View 2 Images Igor Tudor looked to have mistaken Allan Dixon for Arne Slot on Sunday (Image: X)

Igor Tudor seemingly mistook Allan Dixon for Arne Slot ahead of Tottenham Hotspur's away trip to Liverpool on Sunday afternoon. The Croatian headed into the fixture with mounting pressure on his back, having failed to win any of his four matches since taking up an interim managerial role last month.

‌



As things stand, Spurs sit 16th in the Premier League standings on 29 points alongside a 17th-place Nottingham Forest and an 18th West Ham - safe only via goal difference. And with just eight matches remaining in 2025/26, the plausibility of the 47-year-old helping the club avoid their first-ever relegation to the Championship seems to be dwindling by the day.

‌



However, it was Tudor's actions ahead of kick-off against Liverpool that caught the attention of fans worldwide, as he looked to have approached Dixon from behind thinking he was about to share a traditional pre-match handshake with Slot . After making his way out of the tunnel, Tudor tapped Dixon - who is Spurs' player liaison officer - before putting his arm around him and briefly exchanging words.

‌



Tottenham Igor Tudor sack stance update as Liverpool decision made ahead of trip READ MORE :

And one eagle-eyed social media user was quick to share a clip of the interaction online, writing: "Igor Tudor confused Alan Dixon for Arne Slot," with a string of laughing emojis.

The replies section of the post were also filled with sarcastic comments about Tudor's struggles with Spurs, as well as what Dixon was doing on the touchline.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



It comes as Tudor made headlines after his pre-match press conference earlier this week, telling his players that they can either "cry or fight" in their relegation battle. He said: "Not an easy situation, not an easy moment. [It's a] big challenge to change things.

"Like everything in life, you can choose how to see the situation. You can cry or you can fight. You can be the victim or you can change something. This is the message I communicated to the players.

View 2 Images Igor Tudor is struggling with Spurs (Image: Getty Images)

‌



"The bottle is either half empty or half full. Here there is nothing full, there are a lot of empty things. But difficult moments don't last forever.

"It will pass. I believe the players who take this as an opportunity, who stand up with the courage to change these things, will become better people and players afterwards."

Sports.title: What Rory McIlroy told Scottie Scheffler about Masters Champions Dinner - 'Not doing it'
Sports.content:
What Rory McIlroy told Scottie Scheffler about Masters Champions Dinner - 'Not doing it'

Rory McIlroy completed the Career Grand Slam last April by finally clinching the coveted green jacket of the Masters, before taking a subtle jab at Scottie Scheffler by detailing his 2026 menu for the Champions Dinner

View 2 Images Rory McIlroy teased Scottie Scheffler about the Masters Champions Dinner menu (Image: Getty Images for The Showdown)

Rory McIlroy joked that burgers will not be on the Masters Champions Dinner menu for 2026 after snagging his first win at the tournament last April. The Northern Irishman became just the sixth man to complete the Career Grand Slam of winning all four majors when he added a long-awaited green jacket to his collection of accolades at Augusta.

‌



It also landed as McIlroy's first major title in 11 years, adding to his previous wins at the US Open, the Open Championship and the PGA Championship. As a result, McIlroy will oversee a Masters dinner ahead of this year's tournament, where he will be able to pick his own menu and serve up a feast for fellow champions.

‌



In 2025, Scottie Scheffler hosted the event thanks to his own win a year prior, where he opted for cheeseburger sliders 'Scottie style', as well as firecracker shrimp and meatball and ravioli bites. The World No 1 also opted for a Texas-style chilli for starters, as well as a chocolate chip skillet cookie for desert.

‌



Rory McIlroy shoots down conspiracy theory about him at Players Championship READ MORE :

However, after clinching the title with a nail-biting play-off win over Justin Rose last spring, McIlroy was quick to dismiss the idea of a similar menu in 2026. He told Sky Sports: "I told Scottie when I saw him in Butler Cabin, I said I am not serving burgers next year!

"I am not sure, I have a year to think about it. But hopefully I will come up with a unique menu and a menu that all the past champions will enjoy."

While it remains under wraps as to exactly what McIlroy will dish up at the banquet, a number of Irish-themed dishes were touted to feature shortly after his victory last April. William Hill, meanwhile, even went as far as publishing odds for meals that McIlroy could choose.

‌



The favourite from the bookmakers was Irish stew at 2/1, while soda bread, fish and chips and Bushmills whiskey were priced at 3/1. Champ, Irish-style chicken curry and a Yellowman dessert confection were listed at 5/1, while Ardglass potted herring and a dessert of Fifteens were priced at 8/1.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

It comes as Scheffler revealed he will not sit next to Tiger Woods or Jack Nicklaus at this year's dinner due to a protocol in seating arrangements. The two-time champion explained that there are no assigned places; instead, some golfers sit in sections.

‌



He explained at the Arnold Palmer Invitational: "Zach [Johnson] and Jordan [Spieth] always sat next to each other, and I definitely didn't ask Jordan to sit by him, because he would have done something to make sure that I didn't have a place to sit!

"So I kind of looked at Zach, I was like, 'Hey, where are you sitting this year?' And he told me, and he was nice and let me join him. But I definitely wasn't going to ask Jordan for that because he would have messed with me."

View 2 Images Rory McIlroy won last year's Masters in April (Image: Getty Images)

‌



He added: "There's a little protocol. Guys have, like, I would say, sections where they sit. But you move around a little bit. There are no necessarily assigned seats, but I'm definitely not going to sit in the area where Tiger and Jack sit. Like, there are kinds of spots where you feel you'll naturally flow into."

Reflecting on his first time hosting in 2023, Scheffler explained: "Yeah, I think definitely the first time around when you're hosting the dinner, it's your first time in that room, you don't really know what to expect. I had no idea what it was going to be like.

"The only thing I really knew was where I was going to sit. That was basically the only thing I knew, sitting next to Mr Crenshaw and the chairman. So, yeah, I was definitely nervous. I'm not really a super social guy to begin with.

Article continues below

"I got there early, checked everything out, snuck Meredith up there, let her see, because she kind of helped me plan a lot of the food and all that. Obviously, I wanted her to see it, so I snuck her up the back, showed her everything before it got started."

Sports.title: Man Utd chiefs issued clear transfer demand during crucial Aston Villa win
Sports.content:
Man Utd chiefs issued clear transfer demand during crucial Aston Villa win

Manchester United took a significant step towards a spot in the Champions League with victory over Aston Villa with Casemiro once again key to victory for Michael Carrick’s side

View 2 Images Michael Carrick has been sent a clear message over Casemiro's future (Image: Getty Images)

Manchester United fans serenaded Casemiro against Aston Villa, demanding he sign a new deal with the club. The Brazilian midfielder confirmed earlier this year that he would be leaving Old Trafford come the end of the campaign.

‌



There is an option in his contract to extend his stay by a further campaign but that will not be taken up by the club or player. Since then, the veteran midfielder has found some excellent form.

‌



His goal against Aston Villa was his seventh of the season, making it his most productive campaign at Old Trafford. Following United’s 3-1 win, supporters made their feelings known as they chanted ‘one more year, one more year, Casemiro’.

‌



Meanwhile, Casemiro also displayed his affection for the club as he proudly showed the United badge to the Stretford End as part of his celebrations.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Man Utd get immediate Aston Villa goal explanation as Gary Neville spots telling moment READ MORE :

Ironically, former United star Kleberson has suggested that the Brazilian would still prefer to stay. He said: “He won a lot of trophies in Real Madrid, but I think he felt that he finally had the opportunity to play in the best league in the world, when he got the opportunity to play for Man United.

‌



“He is a fantastic midfielder and I even think he has improved at Man United, but Man United is always up and down as a club. That is why he hasn't won that many trophies or has had the opportunity to play the big finals like a Champions League final.

“The philosophy has changed a lot, new managers have come in and players have moved to and away from Man United. Everything has been tough, and it has been completely different from his time at Real Madrid. He didn`t experience many changes at Real Madrid.

View 2 Images Casemiro celebrates his goal againsr Aston Villa (Image: Getty Images)

‌



“I know he cost a lot of money, but he has really brought some leadership to the club. That is also why he came back into the Brazilian national team and why he will play in the World Cup. He really brings the leadership and he does that as well in Man United. He is a great team-mate and a great competitor.

“Of course, we can’t compare his leadership to Roy Keane ’s because that was completely different, but he knows the rules at Man United. I think he is happy at Man United, but the club has never been in a good position. I still think he would like to stay at Man United, or at least he is thinking about it because it has gone so well under Michael Carrick.”

United now sit third and in the table after victory against Villa. They are next back in action on Friday evening as they take on Bournemouth.
